title: Alternance 2026   Analyste Quantitatif - Data Scientist - Validation des Modèles  H/F
description: Qui sommes nous ?Crédit Agricole Personal Finance & Mobility, filiale à 100% du Groupe Crédit Agricole, est un leader du financement personnel et un fournisseur d’accès à toutes les solutions de mobilités en Europe.Il distribue ces solutions en direct auprès des particuliers, sur les lieux de vente ou sur les plateformes e-commerce de ses partenaires, une gamme étendue de solutions de financement. Ces solutions de crédit amortissable, crédit renouvelable, leasing et rachat de crédit - avec des services associés dont les assurances, des solutions de paiement fractionné et des services dédiés à la mobilité, ont pour objectif de répondre aux enjeux de transition énergétique dans la mobilité, l’habitat et la consommation.Rejoindre Crédit Agricole Personal Finance & Mobility, au sein du Crédit Agricole, c’est avoir l’opportunité d’accéder à cet univers très large de métiers et d’expertises.Pour la deuxième fois en France, Crédit Agricole Personal Finance & Mobility est labellisée Best Work Places et se classe parmi les 10 meilleures entreprises de plus de 2 500 salariés où il fait bon travailler.Présentation Du Service:Envie de rejoindre une équipe dynamique et de participer à de beaux projets? Corporate Center recrute un(e) "Analyste Quantitatif - Data Scientist - Validation des Modèles", vous rejoindrez l'entité "Group Internal Validation" dont la mission est d'effectuer des revues indépendantes de modèles développés au sein du Groupe CAPFM (acceptation, recouvrement, comportements, provisionnement, stress test, …)Les modèles revus reposent sur des méthodologies très diverses y compris les approches ‘Data Science’ (forêt aléatoire, modèles de boosting, machines à vecteurs de support, réseaux de neurones, etc.)Descriptif De La Mission :Au sein du département Group Internal Validation, sous la supervision de votre tuteur, vous aurez principalement deux missions :Contribuer à des missions de revue des modèles quantitatifs (revue des hypothèses théoriques, revue des programmes et des données, revue des résultats, construction des modèles challengeurs du type Machine Learning, Modèles Econométriques ou Statistiques, etc)En parallèle des missions de revue, vous travaillerez sur la conception et la construction d'un outil d'automatisation de la production d'un certain nombre d'indicateurs ou de tests statistiques permettant d'optimiser le processus de revue des modèles quantitatifs. Pour cela, vous pourrez être amené à travailler sur des outils tels que Rshiny ou Dash.Ces missions vous permettront d'avoir une vue d'ensemble sur la construction et la validation des modèle quantitatifs dans l'environnement bancaireProfil Recherché:Bac+5Outils Informatiques :PythonLangage RSASPackage Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint)Ecoles Souhaitées :ENSAE Paris, ISFA, Dauphine, Master à Dominance Mathématique AppliquéeFormation:Gestion du risque / finance / statistique / analyse de donnéesEcoles Souhaitées:Ingénieur/MasterLangues:Anglais: IndispensableDurée Souhaitée :1 an

title: Senior Data Analyst - Acquisition
description: Are you looking to have an impact on the daily life of millions of entrepreneurs in France (and tomorrow in Europe)?Are you looking for a work environment that values trust, proactivity, and autonomy?Are our Engineering principles aligned with your vision?Then Pennylane is the right place for you !Our visionWe aim to become the most beloved financial Operating System of French SMEs and Accounting Firms (and soon, European ones).We help entrepreneurs rid themselves of time-consuming tasks related to accounting and finance while providing them with access to key financial information to assist in making the best decisions for their business.About UsPennylane is one of the fastest growing Fintechs in France (and soon to be in Europe!)In 5 years of existence, we’ve managed to :💻 Make ourselves known as a groundbreaking accounting and financial software for small businesses and their accountants💰 Raise a total of €400 millions, including from Sequoia, the famous fund from the Silicon Valley who invested early in companies like Google, Facebook, Airbnb, Stripe, Paypal and much more...👨‍👩‍👧‍👦 Grow from 7 cofounders to 1000 happy Pennylaners : we’re now recognized as one of the greatest places to work in France (and also remotely), with a 4.6/5 rating on Glassdoor.🌍 Build an international environment with more than 25 nationalities, with a strong remote-friendly culture, where 30% of the employees are already working from all parts of Europe🤝 Earn the trust of thousands of customers and accounting firms and obtain outstanding ratings🚀 Already more than 900,000 small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) and over 6000 accounting firms use Pennylane in France!WHY this position is of utmost importance to reach our missionAt Pennylane, every decision that is not backed by data is likely to be challenged (when data is available of course). This culture is embraced by the leadership team, and actively promoted everywhere. The role of the data team is to make this possible by treating data like a production asset, making it available company-wide, and using it proactively to improve Pennylane’s user experience.By joining us as a Senior Data Analyst, you will have a pivotal role in large projects, bringing your business sense and analytical thinking to help us meet our high delivery standards.HOW You Will Contribute To The CompanyAs a Senior Data Analyst, you will be part of the Science & Analytics team focused on Acquisition (5+ people), inside the Data department (40+ people).You will proactively use data to identify opportunities to grow our business or improve our processes.You will be a real sparring partner for Sales & Operation leaders, feed their strategy by anticipating the most important business questions, and leverage your data & statistical skills to answer them.You will steer decision‑making in your business area through rigorous analyses, navigating ambiguity and collaborating across domains when needed.You will drive our self-service approach to analytics, by understanding our colleague’s needs in their use of data, and promoting a data driven culture. The Science & Analytics team is also fully in charge of creating data models in the Data Warehouse (using dbt).You will contribute to shaping the Data Engineering roadmap to deliver the best experience for your colleagues on the data platform.WHAT You Can Expect From Your Life At PennylaneWithin one month:You will learn everything about our company, our teams, and our vision during the first onboarding week.You will get familiar with our stack, and have delivered a few small projects which will give you a concrete taste of our tools & processes.You will be given time to meet your future stakeholders, and gain a deep knowledge of our product and operations.Within 3 months:You will be fully in charge of supporting your area of the business, prioritizing your tasks with decision makers.You will be confortable with our technical stack (Python, Redshift, DBT).You will contribute to larger cross-company projects.Within 6 months:You will proactively contribute to the team’s roadmap.You will work with engineers and data practitioners on improving our stack and data platform.You will share your learnings and best practices within the team.And beyond:the data team will continue growing with the companyWhich means:Opportunities to recruit and mentor new team members,Increased accountability in project leadership,Responsibilities to design and implement new processes, tools and best practices to make sure that your team works even more efficiently.Who are we looking for?You’re the right candidate if you:Have had previous experiences as Data Analyst, ideally in scale-up environments (5-6 years)Are seasoned working with SQL, DBT and data visualization toolsAre comfortable collaborating with cross-functional stakeholders beyond the data teamYou have a strong business acumen and are seen as a trusted partner by your business stakeholdersHave strong communication skills and are capable of articulating your ideas clearlyAre Fluent in English - French is a plusWhat does the recruitment process look like?A first interview with our Talent Acquisition ManagerA case study interview to discuss a topic closely related to one of our priorities (60 min)A past project interview to hear about your experience (60 min)An interview with our Tech & Product leaders to discuss our company culture (45 min)What do we do to make your work life easier🌴 Wherever you are based, you will get 25 vacations days paid by Pennylane💵 You’ll have a competitive compensation package📈 You'll get company shares to enjoy a piece of the success story you're building with us🏡 You’ll have a budget to turn your home into a more comfortable workspace, as well as a monthly allowance to work from a coworking space whenever you feel like it⛹️ Through our partner Gymlib, you’ll have access to 8000 fitness spaces in Europe and more than 300 activities related to wellness🇬🇧 You’ll have access to Busuu to perfect your English or your French💻 You’ll get the latest Apple equipment🏢 Depending on the teams and the requirements of the position - you'll be able to work remotely from your country of residence, as long as it is in Europe and within a maximum time difference of two hours from the CET time zone🎉 We are committed to regularly coming together for company events such as Tech Days (which bring remote Pennylaners together every 3 months) or our annual company seminar, fostering significant moments of cohesion for everyone.If you are based in France, you will have a French contract following French regulation on top of the additional perks : 6 to 12 RTT, 5 weeks PTOs, lunch credits (Swile), Alan Blue healthcare cover and regular events in cities where Pennylaners are mostly presents (Lyon, Bordeaux, Nantes…)We're working on providing those last advantages to our people based outside of France as well, but it can be quite more complex depending on different countries.Who are we looking for ?To thrive at Pennylane, you need :To speak English (level is assessed and appreciated according to the department you’re applying to)To be energized by an ever-shifting work environmentTo be highly collaborative (within your team or other stakeholders)Sufficiently experienced to prioritize business-led actions on your day to day activityWe know that some people are less likely to apply than others, if they don’t feel like they meet the full list of criteria.If you’re hesitating, we encourage you to apply : who knows, it might be the start of a meaningful and long-lasting collaboration.Important information for candidatesRecruitment scam attempts are on the rise. We invite you to remain vigilant during your exchanges.Applications through official channels only: apply exclusively through our job postings published on our official platforms (career site, official partner pages).Always verify the sender's email address: our communications are sent from professional email addresses with the domains @pennylane.com or @pennylane-partners.comWe will never ask you for payment or financial information(bank details, payment for an interview, equipment purchases, etc.) as part of the recruitment process. If such a request is made to you, it is fraudulent. We invite you tonot respondand toreport it to us immediately.We also want to emphasize that we fully embrace diversity, equity and inclusion and that we’re doing our best to create a safe and inclusive environment.We are committed to providing an equal employment opportunity regardless of gender, sexual orientation, origin, disabilities, or any other traits that make you who you are. If anything, diversity makes us a more fun place to work at.

title: Data Analyst Senior H/F
description: En tant queData Analyst Senior h/f, vous rejoindrez l'équipe Pilotage et Analyse Data dont la mission est d'extraire, traiter et exploiter les données afin de piloter nos plans d'action et tirer ainsi des conclusions pertinentes permettant de faciliter la prise de décision.Vous rejoignez une équipe de 6 personnes. Ce poste est basé à Paris.Vos Missions PrincipalesVous participez au développement de cette équipe avec pour mission principale l'élaboration de modélisations, de projections statistiques, d'analyses, et de notes de synthèses permettant de calibrer, piloter nos activités et mesurer l'efficacité de nos plans d’actions.Développer et maintenir des modèles mathématiques de prévisions ou projections des flux pour améliorer le pilotage transverse des activités et calibrer à la perfection nos besoins. Suivre et actualiser les modèles.Collecter l'ensemble des informations (hypothèse, roadmap projet, nouvelles données) nécessaires aux paramétrages des modèles et à leurs évolutions, auprès de nos Directeurs.Mettre en place des suivis d'activité orientés Vision clientDéfinir les KPIs pertinents, explorer des nouvelles sources de données et spécifier nos besoins auprès du décisionnel. Consolider des sources de données déjà pré-transformées en amontDéfinir et mettre en place des analyses avancées et tableaux de bords en lien avec le pilotage des flux, le suivi des performances et plus particulièrement de piloter notre stratégie d’externalisation (prédictions de commandes) et notre transformation digitaleRédiger des notes et livrables clairs synthétiques, et adaptés au destinataire. Documenter vos travaux et privilégier le rationnelEn complément de cette activité de business et data analyse, vous interviendrez sur les missions suivantes :Mener les différents travaux de business analyse, afin de recueillir, comprendre les besoins métiers et les transformer en solution data (analyse, tableau de bord, modèle)Valoriser les tableaux de bord et analyses dans une logique de data visualisation. Vous participez à sensibiliser à la richesse des solutions existantesIntervenir sur les projets transverses au sein de l'équipe et au sein d'Harmonie (datalab, analyse d'impact...)Participer à la sensibilisation des différentes équipes métier à la culture de la dataExpérience SouhaitéeVous avez au moins 7 ans d'expérience professionnelleVous êtes formé en Statistiques / Econométrie / Analyse de donnéesUne connaissance des métiers de la mutuelle serait un plusQualités PersonnellesVous vous considérez comme un mathématicien au service du business, mais savez aussi comprendre ce business et adapter vos livrables à votre public.Vous êtes curieux(se), rigoureux(se) et doté(e) d'un bon esprit d'analyse et de synthèse vous permettant d’exceller en résolution de problèmes. Vous êtes force de proposition, autonome, dynamique, innovant, structuré.Vous aimez le travail en équipe et démontrez des qualités de communication pour partager vos idées et résultats de façon efficace. Vous avez un bon sens relationnel pour communiquer à des niveaux hiérarchiques élevés (Direction).Outils InformatiquesVous êtes à l'aise sous SAS, SQL, Python, Excel et PowerBINos AvantagesAccord d’intéressementTélétravail jusqu'à 3 jours par semaine (à partir de 6 mois d'ancienneté)22,5 jours de RTT par anCarte déjeuner à 9,50 € (60 % pris en charge par l’employeur) et avantages CSE (enveloppes loisirs, culture, avantages vacances…)Forfait mobilité durable : jusqu’à 400 € par an (cumulable avec le remboursement de l’abonnement aux transports en commun, dans la limite de 600 euros au total)Contrat collectif santé et prévoyancePlan d’épargne entreprise, plan d’épargne retraite et compte épargne tempsFormation : un parcours complet avec une plateforme d’apprentissage en ligne2 jours d'engagement solidaire par an au profit d’associations

title: Data Analyst Senior (F/H)
description: A Propos De NousLe saviez-vous ?Créateur de l’hypermarché, Carrefour reste fidèle à ses racines tout en se réinventant pour permettre à chacun de mieux manger : plus sain, plus local, plus responsable.Leader mondial de la distribution, nous sommes engagés pour la diversité, la RSE et la transformation digitale, tout en créant un environnement de travail inclusif et stimulant.Nos atouts pour y parvenir ?Un réseau multi format, multi métiers avec des collaborateurs passionnés, qui s’engagent, pour réussir la transition alimentaire pour tous.Carrefour recherche pour sa Direction Tech & Data basée à Massy (91) un :Data Analyst Senior (F/H)🎯Vos missionsLe data analyst a pour mission de collecter, analyser et interpréter des données pour réaliser des analyses à même d’éclairer les décisions, de guider la mise en place de plans d’actions au plus proche des problématiques business. Il est garant de la mise en œuvre des dispositifs de mesure et joue un rôle important dans l’amélioration de la performance du e-commerce en France.RattachementLe Data Analyst sera rattaché hiérarchiquement au Data Translator au sein du pôle e-commerce et marketing de l’analytics factory et fonctionnellement aux membres du Comité de Direction des équipes business e-commerce France.ResponsabilitésAnalyser les problématiques au croisement entre les Clients (conso style, life stage, etc…), l'Offre (Catégorie, Promo) les canaux de vente (Service, Modèles, format E-commerce) et les analyses de la performance des catégories de produits (CA, marge…) afin d’ajuster l’offre vendue au bon prix,Mettre en place des reportings récurrents sous forme de tableaux de bord pour chaque BU (DRIVE, CLCV...) : détection des ruptures, identifier zone risque (nouveaux, fréquence, churn, panier moyen, etc.),Réaliser des analyses clients sur les services e-commerce (Drive, Livraison…) comme par exemple la construction des équations clients annuelles (recrutement, fréquence, panier) et préconiser des ajustements des investissements en conséquence, des analyses d’élasticité prix pour les services LAD (Livraison à Domicile) et identifier les opportunités d’ajustement de la proposition de valeur (frais de livraison gratuits, ajustement du mark-up, seuils de livraison, etc.) selon les comportements clients observés.👥Votre ProfilEsprit analytique, rigueur, capacité à structurer et esprit de synthèse,Excellente communication et clarté dans la restitution des analyses,Maîtrise solide de SQL (BigQuery, GCP, Tableau, Looker),Réalisation de projets de Data Science et maîtrise de Python est un plus,Capacité à évoluer en mode agile au sein d’une équipe pluridisciplinaire,> 7 ans d’expérience en Conseil, Analyse Clients x Data, Produits de Grande, Consommation, Digital, Commerce de Détail, E-commerce, B-to-C ou Web.📝Informations complémentairesContrat : CDILieu : Siège mondial du groupe Carrefour, Massy, FranceStatut : Cadre - Niveau 8Intéressement et participationTélétravail : Possibilité de bénéficier d'un jour de télétravailMutuelle / prévoyanceRemboursement des frais de transport à hauteur de 50%10-12% de remise sur achats avec la carte PassUn comité d'entreprise dynamique et de perspectives d'évolution au sein du Groupe CarrefourVous aurez accès aux infrastructures du campus (salle de sport, conciergerie, drive, parking et restauration)Carrefour s’engage pour la santé et le bien-être de ses collaborateurs en leur proposant différents services : une solution gratuite pour faire du sport grâce à l'application Gymlib.Vous avez des questions sur cette offre je reste à votre écoute à l'adresse mail suivante : pierre_boivin@carrefour.comChez Carrefour, nous avons à cœur de ne passer à côté d’aucun talent et sommes fiers de compter des équipes représentatives de la société dans son ensemble. Nous encourageons ainsi tous types de profils à postuler à cette offre et garantissons un processus de recrutement dénué de toutes formes de discriminations

title: Senior Data Analyst - Care
description: Are you looking to have an impact on the daily life of millions of entrepreneurs in France (and tomorrow in Europe)?Are you looking for a work environment that values trust, proactivity, and autonomy?Are our Engineering principles aligned with your vision?Then Pennylane is the right place for you !Our visionWe aim to become the most beloved financial Operating System of French SMEs and Accounting Firms (and soon, European ones).We help entrepreneurs rid themselves of time-consuming tasks related to accounting and finance while providing them with access to key financial information to assist in making the best decisions for their business.About UsPennylane is one of the fastest growing Fintechs in France (and soon to be in Europe!)In 5 years of existence, we’ve managed to :💻 Make ourselves known as a groundbreaking accounting and financial software for small businesses and their accountants💰 Raise a total of €400 millions, including from Sequoia, the famous fund from the Silicon Valley who invested early in companies like Google, Facebook, Airbnb, Stripe, Paypal and much more...👨‍👩‍👧‍👦 Grow from 7 cofounders to 1000 happy Pennylaners : we’re now recognized as one of the greatest places to work in France (and also remotely), with a 4.6/5 rating on Glassdoor.🌍 Build an international environment with more than 25 nationalities, with a strong remote-friendly culture, where 30% of the employees are already working from all parts of Europe🤝 Earn the trust of thousands of customers and accounting firms and obtain outstanding ratings🚀 Already more than 900,000 small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) and over 6000 accounting firms use Pennylane in France!WHY this position is of utmost importance to reach our missionAt Pennylane, every decision that is not backed by data is likely to be challenged (when data is available of course). This culture is embraced by the leadership team, and actively promoted everywhere. The role of the data team is to make this possible by treating data like a production asset, making it available company-wide, and using it proactively to improve Pennylane’s user experience.By joining us as a Senior Data Analyst, you will have a pivotal role in large projects, bringing your business sense and analytical thinking to help us meet our high delivery standards.HOW You Will Contribute To The CompanyAs a Senior Data Analyst, you will be part of the Science & Analytics team focused on Customer Care (3-5 people), inside the Data department (40+ people).You will proactively use data to identify opportunities to improve customer and partner outcomes and our Care processes.You will be a real sparring partner for Care leaders (Partner Success and Customer Success), feed their strategy by anticipating the most important customer and partner questions, and leverage your data and statistical skills to answer them.You will steer decision‑making in your business area through rigorous analyses, navigating ambiguity and collaborating across domains when needed.You will drive our self-service approach to analytics, by understanding our colleague’s needs in their use of data, and promoting a data driven culture. The Science & Analytics team is also fully in charge of creating data models in the Data Warehouse (using dbt).You will contribute to shaping the Data Engineering roadmap to deliver the best experience for your colleagues on the data platform.WHAT You Can Expect From Your Life At PennylaneWithin one month:You will learn everything about our company, our teams, and our vision during the first onboarding week.You will get familiar with our stack, and have delivered a few small projects which will give you a concrete taste of our tools & processes.You will be given time to meet your future stakeholders, and gain a deep knowledge of our product and operations.Within 3 months:You will be fully in charge of supporting your area of the business, prioritizing your tasks with decision makers.You will be confortable with our technical stack (Python, Redshift, DBT).You will contribute to larger cross-company projects.Within 6 months:You will proactively contribute to the team’s roadmap.You will work with engineers and data practitioners on improving our stack and data platform.You will share your learnings and best practices within the team.And beyond:the data team will continue growing with the companyWhich means:Opportunities to recruit and mentor new team members,Increased accountability in project leadership,Responsibilities to design and implement new processes, tools and best practices to make sure that your team works even more efficiently.Who are we looking for?You’re the right candidate if you:Have had previous experiences as Data Analyst, ideally in scale-up environments (5-6 years)Are seasoned working with SQL, DBT and data visualization toolsAre comfortable collaborating with cross-functional stakeholders beyond the data teamYou have a strong business acumen and are seen as a trusted partner by your business stakeholdersHave strong communication skills and are capable of articulating your ideas clearlyAre Fluent in English - French is a plusWhat does the recruitment process look like?A first interview with our Talent Acquisition ManagerA case study interview to discuss a topic closely related to one of our priorities (60 min)A past project interview to hear about your experience (60 min)An interview with our Tech & Product leaders to discuss our company culture (45 min)

title: Senior Data Analyst
description: Nous recherchons un Sr Data Analyst pour renforcer notre équipe Data. Ce rôle clé consiste à garantir la fiabilité, la performance et la valeur de nos données marketing et programmatiques. Le poste se situe à l’intersection de l’analyse de données, de l’analyse de performance et de la publicité digitale (programmatique, DOOH, DSP, etc.). Vous accompagnerez les équipes commerciales, opérationnelles et produit dans leurs prises de décisions stratégiques et leurs optimisations quotidiennes.Responsabilités principales1. Développement opérationnel et optimisation techniqueÉvaluer la faisabilité et définir les méthodologies pour les offres de campagnes sur mesure.Suivre la diffusion des campagnes, détecter et résoudre les anomalies, et assurer une optimisation continue.Gérer les flux de données provenant de divers partenaires (fournisseurs de mesure, fournisseurs de données, etc.).2. Analyse et reportingRéaliser des analyses ad-hoc internes et externes.Créer et automatiser des tableaux de bord de performance pour les différentes équipes.Superviser l’exactitude et la cohérence des rapports existants et assurer les corrections nécessaires.Produire les rapports financiers mensuels, incluant les transactions exceptionnelles.Mettre en place des systèmes de monitoring et d’alerte pour garantir la qualité et la fraîcheur des données.3. Collaboration et stratégieCollaborer avec les équipes Ops, Produit, Tech et Sales pour aligner les livrables data avec les besoins business.Contribuer à la définition des KPIs clés et à leur intégration dans les outils analytiques.Contribuer à la veille technologique et à l’innovation.Aider à accroître la maturité de l’entreprise concernant les pratiques liées à l’IA.Formation & ExpérienceMaster en Data Science, Informatique, Statistiques ou équivalent.Minimum 5 ans d’expérience en Data Analytics (en plus des stages et alternances).Une expérience dans la publicité programmatique ou le marketing digital est un atout majeur.Compétences techniquesExcellente maîtrise de SQL (BigQuery) et Python.Forte expertise sur les outils de modélisation de données, idéalement dbt.Solides connaissances d’Airflow, des architectures ETL/ELT et de la gestion de pipelines.Compréhension de la publicité programmatique.Maîtrise des outils de visualisation (Looker Studio, Tableau ou équivalent).Expérience des environnements Cloud (GCP, AWS ou équivalent).Forte compréhension des modèles de performance publicitaire.Soft Skills (Qualités professionnelles)Grandes capacités d’analyse et esprit critique.Agilité et solides pratiques de développement.Autonomie, rigueur et sens des priorités.Capacité à vulgariser des concepts techniques pour des équipes non techniques.Excellente communication et esprit collaboratif.Proactivité et attitude orientée solutions.Unpremier échange téléphoniqueavec la HR & Talent Acquisition pour faire connaissance et comprendre votre parcours.Unevisio avec le Manager Analytics ou la Directrice Analyticspour parler du poste, des missions et de quelques sujets techniques.Untest technique dans nos bureauxavec la Directrice Analytics : l’occasion de se rencontrer en vrai, découvrir les bureaux et tester vos compétences.

title: Senior Data Analyst / Analytics Engineer
description: À propos d’EmeriaEmeria est le leader mondial des services et technologies immobilières, offrant des prestations aux particuliers et aux entreprises. Emeria accompagne les clients résidentiels et commerciaux à chaque étape de leur parcours immobilier avec une gamme complète de services compétitifs : acquisition, location, gestion de copropriété et transactions. Le Groupe opère principalement en Europe, dans 8 pays, avec une stratégie de multi-marques (Foncia, Constatimmo, Esset…).A propos de l’équipe AnalyticsL’équipe Analytics fait partie du pôle Data Science, qui est en charge de l’IA et de la data du groupe Emeria.L'équipe Analytics pilote l’ensemble de la data du groupe, centralise les informations issues d'Emeria Technologies et des autres filiales, et crée les dashboards et analyses qui éclairent les décisions stratégiques.L’équipe fonctionne comme une start-up dans le groupe : esprit agile, autonomie forte, collaboration étroite avec les métiers, et une vraie culture du partage et de la curiosité.Tes missionsEn tant qu'Analytics Engineer senior, tu es au cœur de la transformation de la donnée : de son ingestion dans notre Datalake (Snowflake) jusqu’à la création de dashboards clairs et percutants, accessibles à plus de 10 000 collaborateurs.Ton objectif ? Transformer la donnée brute en insights actionnables, pour que chaque décision dans l’entreprise repose sur des faits concrets et à jour.Tes Missions Sont Notamment Les SuivantesTu es le Business Partner Data des équipes métiers :Comprendre et traduire les besoins : tu collabores avec les équipes métiers (Immobilier, Finance, Marketing, Comptabilité…) pour comprendre leurs enjeux, formaliser leurs besoins et les transformer en solutions data concrètes.Donner du sens aux chiffres : tu ne te contentes pas de produire des dashboards, tu comprends ce qu’ils racontent. Tu mets en perspective les données, tu identifies les tendances et tu aides les équipes à prendre les bonnes décisions.Industrialiser les analyses : pas d’analyses « one shot » ici. Chaque dashboard, chaque extraction est pensée pour être réutilisable et scalable. Tu centralises les besoins, tu optimises les existants et tu rends chaque insight accessible à tous, tout le temps.Collaborer avec les équipes techniques : tu travailles main dans la main avec les Data Scientists, DevOps et autres experts pour garantir la fiabilité, la performance et la qualité des données.Tu conçois etfais vivre les modèles de données:Tu es garant(e) de la solidité et de la fiabilité de notre architecture data. Tu construis, fais évoluer et optimises les modèles qui alimentent nos dashboards et outils analytiques, pour que chaque donnée serve vraiment à quelque chose.Construire & faire tourner la machine :Build→ tu conçois de nouveaux pipelines, dashboards et outils data pour répondre aux besoins métiers et soutenir la croissance du groupe.Run→ tu assures la maintenance, corriges les éventuels bugs, analyses les incidents et fais évoluer les pipelines existants pour qu’ils restent performants et fiables.Modéliser intelligemment la donnée : tu structures et optimises les modèles de données pour le reporting et l’analyse. Tu penses architecture avant tout : comment stocker la donnée de manière efficace, durable et exploitable, tout en conservant l’historique nécessaire à une analyse fine.Garantir la qualité et la fiabilité : tu veilles à ce que chaque chiffre communiqué soit juste. Tu mets en place les bonnes pratiques de gouvernance et de contrôle qualité pour assurer l’intégrité de la donnée dans toute l’entreprise.Rester à la pointe : tu assures une veille technologique continue pour identifier les nouvelles tendances et améliorer nos process, nos outils et nos pratiques.ProfilTu as au moins 2 ans d’expérience en tant qu’Analytics Engineer ou Data Analyst, et tu maîtrises les bases de la donnée de bout en bout.À l’aise avec les problématiques d’industrialisation de code, tu sais modéliser la donnée et construire des pipelines robustes (DBT ou équivalent).Tu es force de proposition : tu identifies les points d’amélioration et n’hésites pas à challenger l’existant.Autonome et débrouillard(e), tu vas chercher l’info, tu explores la data et tu plonges volontiers dans le code pour comprendre ce qu’il s’y passe.La communication, c’est ton autre super-pouvoir : tu sais vulgariser un sujet technique pour le rendre clair et accessible à tous.Curieux(se) de nature, tu t’intéresses aux enjeux business et tu poses toujours les bonnes questions pour donner du sens aux chiffres.Tu maîtrises le SQL sur le bout des doigts et un outil de visualisation (Tableau est un vrai plus).Ton esprit analytique et logique te permet de structurer ta pensée et d’aller droit au but.Clair(e), concis(e), pédagogue : tu sais partager tes connaissances et rendre la donnée compréhensible, même pour un public non technique.Excellente maîtrise de l’analyse et de la modélisation de données : tu comprends comment transformer des chiffres en leviers concrets.BonusUne expérience avec Python pour automatiser ou enrichir tes analyses.Une bonne connaissance de Snowflake et/ou de l’environnement AWS.À l’aise avec Git et les bonnes pratiques de versionning.Process De Recrutementune visio RH de 30min avec Mathieu, notre Talent Acquisitionune visio d’1h avec David, notre Lead Data Analyst afin de discuter de ton profil, tes motivations et te présenter notre équipe pour voir ce que nous pouvons t’offrirun entretien technique d’1h dans nos locaux avec deux personnes de l’équipe data pour tester ta logique et ton SQLune visio avec Florent le Chief Data Officer 30min

title: Senior Product Data Analyst
description: TapNation - Redefining Mobile Gaming Excellence🎮TapNation is a French company founded in 2019, specializing in mobile game publishing. In six years, it has released more than 50 games and achieved over a billion downloads, ranking among the top 15 publishers worldwide thanks to a strategy combining creativity and technological optimization.The company prioritizes creativity, innovation, and a dynamic multicultural environment, while offering its employees a strong impact on performance, sustained personal development, and a good balance between performance and well-being (Great Place to Work and Happy at Work certifications).To continue our amazing mission, we are seeking an ambitious and results-driven Senior Product Data Analyst (all type of contracts) to join our Tech team 💪Role Overview👀As Senior Product Data Analyst, you will be the core analytical partner for our Product teams, driving the data-informed evolution of our mobile games and internal tools. Your primary mission will be to ensure the Product team has access toefficient and trustworthy data insights, enabling them to make optimal decisions on features, monetization, and user experience. You will be instrumental in translating complex product and user behavior data into clear, actionable strategies.Your missions:Product Performance Analysis: Conduct deep-dive data analysis to understand user behavior, feature adoption, monetization drivers, and overall game performance (e.g., retention, engagement, conversion).Data Visualization & Reporting: Design, develop, and maintain efficient and trustworthy dashboards (primarily in Looker) to monitor key Product KPIs, providing the Product team with self-service, real-time insights.Experimentation Support: Partner with the Product team to design, execute, and analyze A/B tests and other experiments, ensuring statistical rigor and clear interpretation of results.Consultation & Strategy: Proactively identify opportunities for product improvement and provide data-driven recommendations to inform the product roadmap and strategy.Data Infrastructure Collaboration: Work closely with Data Engineering to ensure data accuracy, integrity, and accessibility for product analysis.Gen AI Exploration: Act as an enthusiast for Generative AI, exploring how emerging AI technologies can be leveraged to enhance product analysis workflows or contribute to future product features.Benefits if freelancer:27 days paid time off per year without loosing fees (the company believes in disconnection in order to allow people to evolve in a healthy environment).Remote policy: full remote.You can find us at Morning Pereire offices in Paris 17e (including fitness room) if you want to say hello.Profile:Experience: Proven, senior-level experience as a Data Analyst, specifically supporting a Product team.Data Analysis: Deep data analysis skills with the ability to structure, analyze, and interpret complex data to provide clear, actionable insights.Industry Concepts: Strong familiarity with mobile gaming industry concepts and metrics (e.g., DAU, MAU, ARPDAU, LTV, Retention Curves, Funnels).SQL Mastery: Expert proficiency in SQL for complex querying and data manipulation.Data Warehouse: Hands-on experience with Google Cloud BigQuery (or similar cloud data warehouses).Transformation: Proficiency with DBT (data build tool) for data transformation, modeling, and maintaining data quality.Visualization: Expert in data visualization and dashboarding using tools such as Looker (preferred), Tableau, or Power BI.Communication: English native or fluent—excellent written and oral communication skills to present complex findings to non-technical stakeholders.TapNation is an equal-opportunity employer🫶Qualified applicants will receive considerationfor employment without regard to sex, gender identity, gender expression, sexual orientation, race, color, religion, national origin, disability, protected veteran status, age, or any other characteristic protected by law.If you're intrigued by the job offer, we encourage you to apply without hesitation, even if you don't meet all the criteria!

title: Senior Data Analyst
description: About Dreem Health, By SunriseDreem Health is America's leading digital sleep clinic, focused on fixing the broken sleep care journey. We connect patients with sleep specialists through a straightforward telehealth platform, eliminating lengthy wait times and complicated in-lab testing. Our clinicians diagnose sleep disorders using home-based tests and deliver effective treatment plans that patients can easily follow. We do this with the pace and creativity of a startup, and the focus of a team that knows every improvement in care makes a difference for patients.Dreem Health is part of the Sunrise Group, a breakthrough technology company that's revolutionizing sleep care with innovative diagnostic and treatment technologies, including a home sleep test that's changing how sleep apnea is diagnosed. Together, we're tackling one of healthcare's biggest challenges: helping the 1+ billion people affected by sleep disorders get the care they deserve. With 85+ team members across the U.S. and Europe, and more than $50M in venture funding from leading investors including Amazon’s Alexa Fund, Eurazeo, Kurma, and VIVES, we are scaling our vision of making great sleep available to everyone.Your OpportunityWe’re hiring a Senior Data Analyst to play a key role in scaling our digital sleep clinic. As the first and only dedicated Data Analyst, you’ll own the analytics function end-to-end — turning raw data into actionable insights that shape our product, operations, and growth strategy.You won’t be starting from scratch: we already have a modern data stack in place, built by our Head of Data & Software (your future manager), who will support you as you take full ownership of the analytics function.From mapping how patients move through our digital clinic to optimizing how doctors and nurses work, you’ll use your analytical mindset and business intuition to drive smarter, faster decisions that create impact across the company.Our tech stack: Python, PostgreSQL, BigQuery, Airflow, Apache Superset, Airbyte, Posthog.Our challenges: We’re scaling quickly. Every week brings new strategic and operational questions that demand clear, data-backed answers. Your mission will be to connect the dots between patient care, operational efficiency, and business performance — building trust in the data, delivering actionable insights, and embedding analytics at the heart of decision-making.We value a friendly, open, and flexible culture, where remote collaboration across time zones is built on trust and autonomy. Our aim: to innovate while enjoying our work and protecting a healthy work-life balance. Our medical staff (doctors, nurses, admins) are based in the US, while our leadership, product, and tech teams are in France and Belgium.As Senior Data Analyst, Here's What You'll DoBI tool Development : Scale, Improve, and maintain BI services using Python, PostgreSQL and related technologies.Operation and Growth Analysis : Understand the full “equation” of our company, identify bottlenecks and levers, and propose optimizations to improve efficiency..Product Analysis : Analyse our user experience at every step of the journey to identify friction points, quantify opportunities, and guide product decisions that improve patient care and engagement.Financial Analysis: Dig into our revenue and margin to help us prioritise actions with the highest business impact.Collaboration : collaborate closely with cross-functional teams to gather requirements, provide technical insights, and deliver solutions that align with business objectives.What You BringProven ability to translate business questions into analytical frameworks and deliver insights that drive actionExperience with Python (especially for data manipulation and pipeline work)Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a fast-paced startup environmentStrong communication skills and the ability to explain complex technical concepts to non-technical stakeholdersBachelor's degree in Computer Science or related field (or equivalent work experience4 to 10 years experience as a Data Analyst, ideally in high-growth or operationally complex environments+3 years experience in PostgreSQLLanguage : French + EnglishWhat Makes You Stand OutExperience with Apache SupersetExperience with Posthog or similar event/product analytics toolsInterest in HealthCare and patient-centered innovationPrevious experience in startups or scaleupsMore than just skills, we're looking for someone engaged and passionate about transforming sleep care with us.About Our TeamAt Sunrise Group, we’re tackling real sleep health challenges that affect millions. We keep things clear and simple ✨, value trust and collaboration 🤝, and lead with optimism and compassion 🌞. These values guide everything we do. If you’re looking for the autonomy to take ownership of your work, make an impact, and be part of a team where ideas move fast and everyone has a voice, Sunrise might just be the right fit.What We OfferMeaningful work that directly improves patients' livesBe part of an international team across the US, Paris, Belgium, and ViennaComprehensive health coverageGenerous vacation allowance + public holidaysFlexible working arrangementsOffice perks and team eventsWe hire humans, not bullet points. Don’t meet every single qualification? That’s okay. We care more about who you are than what’s on your CV. We’re looking for people who are curious, resourceful, and ready to roll up their sleeves — especially if you’re excited about building something new in healthcare. So if you think you could make an impact here, reach out to us.Dreem Health / Sunrise is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We welcome people of all backgrounds and are committed to building a workplace where everyone feels included and respected. We do not tolerate discrimination or harassment of any kind.

title: Senior Product Data Analyst
description: Our mission and customers:We are creating the freedom for SMEs to succeed by delivering Europe's leading finance workspace with banking at its core, augmented by financial tools. We are proud to berated 4.8 on Trustpilot,based on 55,000+ reviews.Our cultureputs customer satisfaction at the core of what we do, as proven by our Net Promoter Score of 75.Our journey:Founded in 2017 byAlexandreandSteve,Qonto has grown to 1,600+ Qontoers serving over 600,000+ customers across 8 European countries. We have been profitable since 2023, and we are just getting started.Our beliefs:We hire for skills and potential. With 80+ nationalities, 45% women, and 56% of women in our leadership team, diversity isn't a program; It's who we are.We've built a discrimination-free hiring processbecause the best teams are built on merit.AI at Qonto:AI is deeply embedded in how we work (here)-Every Qontoer gets unlimited access to the best AI tools. We want people who experiment without waiting for permission, push AI beyond the obvious, know when to trust it, and when to question it.Join us as a Senior Product Analyst and help Qonto’s teams make data-informed decisions as we scale to serve one million SMEs by 2026. Depending on your profile and preferences, you will join our Product Analytics team, partnering with Product Managers and Product teams across our product portfolio.You will work closely with an experienced manager, Ida, Senior Manager Product Analytics, and report to Daniele, Head of Data. They will support you in delivering high-impact analytics work and growing your career within a large, structured team.👩‍💻🧑‍💻 As a Data Analyst at Qonto, you willAct as a strategic partner to Product Managers: You will go beyond execution to challenge metrics, influence product roadmaps, and shape our overall strategy.Champion a product experimentation culture: Drive rigorous A/B testing frameworks from design to impact measurement, ensuring data-informed decisions are at the heart of our product development.Decode complex user behavior: Master the analysis of user journeys and deep funnel dynamics to uncover hidden drop-off points and identify major opportunities for conversion and growth.Own your product domain end-to-end: Take autonomous charge of identifying customer problems, setting up the right telemetry, and delivering actionable recommendations. Your goal is not just to build a dashboard, but to ensure adoption and drive tangible business outcomes.Leverage a best-in-class data ecosystem: Seamlessly blend frontend tracking and backend events using our modern stack (Snowflake, Metabase, Amplitude, Cursor) to paint a complete, actionable picture of product performance.🤔 What You Can ExpectProduct Analytics: You will support Product stakeholders (Product Managers, Designers, Heads of Product). The team is in a consolidation phase, spending 50-60% of time on BI work and moving toward more strategic analysis work (A/B testing, discovery, impact measurement). You will help product teams understand user behavior and validate product decisions.How we work: We follow the Qonto Way methodology, which emphasizes one-piece flow, value analysis, and value engineering. You will work in close partnership with your stakeholders, attending team meetings and building trusted relationships. We value ownership: success means not just publishing a dashboard, but ensuring it drives business value.Current focus: The team is working on improving data discoverability and self-service capabilities, consolidating dashboards to reduce sprawl, and building scalable analytics solutions that drive measurable business impact.🤝 About Your Future ManagerIda manages the Product Analytics team and brings deep expertise in building analytics capabilities in fast-growing tech companies. She is passionate about enabling product teams through data and focuses on helping her team members grow their impact and autonomy.Ida reports toDaniele, VP of Data, who oversees the entire Analytics function and ensures alignment across teams.🏅 About YouTechnical Data Mastery: You are highly proficient in complex SQL and modern data warehouses. You also have strong BI experience (building actionable dashboards in tools like Metabase, Tableau, or Looker) and deeply understand data visualization best practices.Product Analytics Expertise: You bring at least 4 years of experience using product analytics platforms (like Amplitude or Mixpanel) to decode user behavior.Business Acumen & True Ownership: You understand the "why" behind product decisions. You do not settle for "dashboard published"; you actively follow up with stakeholders to ensure your insights are adopted and drive tangible business outcomes.Project & Stakeholder Management: You thrive in a fast-paced environment. You are highly autonomous in breaking down complex projects, managing competing priorities, and communicating proactively to build trust across teams.Language: You are fluent in English.At Qonto we understand that true diversity isn't just about ticking boxes on a hiring checklist. Apply regardless of the boxes you tick! Who knows? You may have the missing piece of the puzzle we've been searching for all along.On average,our hiring process lasts20 working days.More information on our candidate journeyhere🔒 Your security matters to usRecruitment scams are on the rise. Keep in mind, we will never work with third-party platforms or agencies that request payment from candidates.If you receive a suspicious message claiming to be from Qonto, please report it right away (support@qonto.com)

title: Senior/Staff Data Analyst – Care Analytics
description: Hi, we’re Back Market.We’re here to helpmake tech reliable, affordable, and better than new. We're a global marketplace for refurbished devices, helping lower our collective environmental impact by providing trustworthy, affordable tech with 92% less carbon emissions than new.Yep, you read that right. Turns out refurbished tech is way better for the planet than new. In fact, With every device purchased on Back Market, our positive impact on the planet grows. From our Customer Care representatives to our software engineer, every individual at Back Market cuts the planet — and consumers — a break.Our mission is simple: to do more with what we already have.Are you ready to join us?Join our Data, Analytics & Insights team as we sabotage the "new device at all costs" mentality — one customer care insight at a time. You'll be working alongside product teams, care operations, and finance leadership to turn mountains of contact data into actionable intelligence that reduces friction, builds trust, and keeps customers coming back. This role is about making our customer care experience so good that it becomes a competitive advantage in the fight for circular commerce.We're looking for someone who can own the entire analytical problem space for Care & Shipping — not just answer one-off questions, but shape the questions we ask and how decisions get made.YOUR MISSION (IF YOU ACCEPT IT): 🏅Care AnalyticsOwn the measurement of customer satisfaction across all care channels — diagnosing root causes of dissatisfaction, quantifying their downstream impact on retention and trust, and delivering operational insights that drive real improvements in BPO performance and agent management.Build and maintain rigorous evaluation frameworks for AI-powered care tools (chatbots, automated resolution, agent assist) — measuring deflection rates, resolution quality, and cost-per-contact to ensure our automation investments actually deliver.Translate care contact patterns into product root-cause insights that directly inform roadmap priorities, reducing Contact Rate (LTCR) and improving Perfect Order Rate (POR).Develop seller-facing performance analytics to help Back Market's marketplace partners meet our quality charter — because great aftersales experience doesn't just happen on our side.Partner with Product and Care Operations to design and evaluate experiments that quantify the impact of product improvements on care demand.Leadership & CraftSet the analytical standard for the team — defining methodology, championing rigorous experimentation, and helping the wider analytics function grow its craft.Mentor and support more junior analysts, acting as the technical and methodological anchor for the Care & Shipping Analytics team.Partner directly with Care, Product, and Finance leadership — not just delivering analyses, but shaping the questions we ask and how decisions get made.🛠️ OUR TECH STACKHere's What The Team Works With Day-to-dayData Warehouse: Google BigQueryTransformation: dbtVisualisation, Analysis & BI: Omni, AmplitudeLanguages: SQL (essential), Python (very useful)Product & Care Tooling: Amplitude, Zendesk, IntercomExperimentation: Internal A/B testing platform and incrementality frameworksYOU ARE IN THE RIGHT PLACE IF: ⭐We're looking for depth over breadth. These are the must-haves:Strong SQL proficiency and comfort working with large, complex datasets — you can build the query and explain what the result means for the business in the same breath.A rigorous analytical mindset: you know when a correlation is causal, you design experiments rather than just observe outcomes, and you're honest about the limits of the data.A track record of operating at Staff or domain-owner level — you've owned record of operating at Staff or domain-owner level — you've owned entire analytical problem spaces end-to-end, influenced strategy, and delivered outcomes that shaped business direction, not just answered one-off questions, or equivalent hands-on experience.Excellent communication and storytelling skills: you can distill a complex, multi-variable analysis into a crisp narrative that moves the most senior stakeholders to act — without losing the important nuances.Experience working in a customer-facing operations domain — customer care, logistics, marketplace, e-commerce, or similar. You understand that behind every metric is a real person's experience.Nice To HaveExperience designing experiments in operational contexts — A/B tests, holdouts, or incrementality measurement — especially for evaluating the impact of automation or AI tools.Familiarity with customer care platforms (Zendesk, Intercom) or hands-on shipping/logistics analytics.Proficiency in Python for more advanced statistical work, modelling, or analytical automation.WHY SHOULD YOU JOIN US ? ✌🏼At Back Market, we’re committed to hiring and supporting diverse teams of people from all backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives — it’s one of the reasons we’re such a high-scoring certified B Corp company (93.2).BenefitsNo matter your role and seniority level, you’ll enjoy impact-driven work with hands-on career development in an innovative, driven, and fast-paced environment — with benefits to match, like:A mission driven work environment where your day to day makes an impact on the planet. Seriously.Hybrid work environment, with 2 remote days a week and 1 remote work week per quarter, plus 3 flex days.Employee Resource Groups, including mentorship programs, comprehensive accessibility policies, and cultural competency training.At Back Market, we strive to create a workplace that embodies the world we’re trying to change. We’ve embedded our diversity, equity, and inclusion principles into our DNA — from dedicated staff to employee resource groups to our company values.We know that the perfect background for a role doesn’t mean the perfect fit — we encourage you to apply for a role even if you think you may not have all the qualifications.If reasonable accommodations are needed for the interview process, please do not hesitate to discuss this with the Talent Acquisition Team.

title: Senior Data Analyst
description: AboutSwan is the leading European fintech specialized in embedded banking. A laureate of the French government’s selective year list of tech champions, “French Tech Next 120,” we are quickly expanding across Europe.Over 100 European companies have already seamlessly integrated banking services such as accounts, cards, and payments into their products through our Banking-as-a-Service platform. This approach, known as “embedded finance”, empowers businesses to elevate their offerings and create new revenue streams — often times tripling their revenue!Our founders’ vision from the beginning was Simplicity: that anyone with a fintech idea at 2 am can visit our website, start building, and buy themselves a coffee with a self-issued card the very next day. That vision has taken us far.Following a 38M€ Series B round last September, we have been doubling our headcount and already have over 230 Swanees across offices in Paris, Bordeaux, Berlin, Barcelona, Amsterdam, and Milan! If you’re excited about joining a European fintech adventure, Swan is the way!To learn more about us:About Swan;Our story.Job DescriptionSwan is looking for a Data Analyst to join our Data team. The current team is led by Romain, our VP data, and consists of 9 members including Data Engineers, Analytic Engineers and Data Analysts.You will work closely with your stakeholders and become a key partner to boost their impact thanks to data driven decisions and in-depth analyses. Additionally, you will play a key role in data operations by actively contributing to data projects aimed at defining best practices (e.g., dashboard lifecycle management), managing internal initiatives (e.g., implementation of a data catalog with maintenance process definition), and regularly communicating progress on these projects to the rest of the company.✨Your missionDashboard & Reporting development: Build and maintain essential dashboards using Metabase and Google and reports in Google Sheet linked to your assigned domain, ensuring they provide clear visibility into key performance indicators.In-Depth Analysis & Strategic Insights: Conduct complex, deep-dive analyses to uncover trends, patterns, and opportunities that support strategic decision-making for your stakeholders, translating data into clear, actionable recommendations.Data Mart Development: Build high-quality, reusable data marts following guidelines defined by our data platform team, ensuring consistency, scalability, and adherence to best practices.Stakeholder Training & Enablement: Train stakeholders on KPIs and how to interpret the data, empowering them to make data-driven decisions independently.Data Operations & Internal Projects: Lead and contribute to internal data initiatives aimed at improving efficiency, data quality, and best practices across the analytics team (e.g., dashboard lifecycle management, data catalog implementation, process documentation).Preferred experience✨You’re a great match if:Minimum of 6-7 years of experience in Data analytics.Strong proficiency in SQL and familiarity with BI tools.Preferably, experience in working with DBT.Proven track record of implementing data best practices within a teamDemonstrated ability to effectively manage projects and engage with stakeholders.Strong communication and collaboration skills.A commitment to team spirit, transparency, and trustworthiness is important, as we value a collaborative and supportive company culture.Autonomy, a "Can-do" spirit, and the ability to take initiative and ownership of your work.Passion, curiosity, and the capacity to learn quickly are important, as we are constantly exploring new technologies and approaches to improve our work.It’s okay if you don’t tick all the boxes — don’t let imposter syndrome prevent you from applying! 🙌Swan is committed to providing a caring work environment for all employees, regardless of age, sex, disability, sexual orientation, race, religion, or belief.When it comes to recruitment, we’re interested in your work experience, skills, and overall personality. Because diversity makes the workplace stronger and is necessary for Swan’s success, we are intensifying efforts to incorporate concrete actions to help us improve in this area.About SwanMeal Vouchers: We provide a Swile card to cover your meals on work days. 🥗Holidays : 25 days + RTT 🏝️Transport: Monthly mobility package for employees. In accordance with the company agreement for sustainable mobilities, you can now use your mobility package to pay for alternative commuting modes. 🚇Health insurance (mutuelle): Alan. This is Swan's health and welfare insurance. 🚑Sports: Thanks to our partnership with Classpass and Gymlib, you can enjoy advantageous discounts on subscriptions. They offer a wide range of sports activities as well as wellness activities. The offers are valid in several European cities, as well as online. 🏋Hybrid remote policy: We offer the possibility of working from home for 2 days per week. 🏡Offsite: Once a year we gather to reconnect, deep-dive into big topics, and relax. 🤝This isn’t a perk, it should be the rule, but diversity and inclusion are important at Swan. We’re working hard to get better every day.✨Our values:Swan’s core values guide our actions daily. Individually, they may seem obvious, but together, they form a unique culture.Simplicity:Leonardo Da Vinci said: “simplicity is the ultimate sophistication.” If something's convoluted or confusing, we work extra hard to break it down. - Making complex things simple is what we do.Long Term: We always play the long game, whether it's to support our partners in their growth journey, or make tangible commitments to climate action.Excellence: We are a team of experts who consistently go all out to create pixel-perfect banking services and exceed our partners' expectations— whatever it takes.Be Human: We believe in the power of kindness and the importance of acting with integrity. But embracing our humanity extends beyond interpersonal interactions, it means caring about greater issues that affect our planet.You can find out more about our culture.Recruitment processPrescreen with Talent AcquisitionPeer/Culture fit interviewTechnical InterviewHiring Manager interview

title: Senior Product Data Analyst
description: About ShineShine exists to help freelancers and small business owners reclaim the joy of working for themselves.Running a business shouldn't mean drowning in financial admin – it should be inspiring and rewarding. Our software brings banking, invoicing, accounting and admin together in one place, so entrepreneurs can focus on what matters most: growing their business and enjoying the freedom of working for themselves.We're a multicultural team of over 500 people across France, Germany, Denmark and the Netherlands. By bringing together leading European fintechs like Shine, Kontist and Tellow, we've built a single, intuitive platform designed for simplicity, speed and accuracy – backed by local, award-winning support.Your Hiring Experience MattersJust as we respect our customers' time, we respect yours. Your experience with Shine should feel simple, transparent and genuinely supportive.If this sounds like somewhere you want to grow, we'd love to hear from you.Data at ShineOur Data team is organised into three complementary pillars: Data Engineering, Analytics Engineering, and Product/Revenue Analytics. This structure ensures you spend less time fixing broken pipelines and more time uncovering high-value growth levers.The Product Analytics team partners closely with Product to drive discovery, experimentation, and measurable impact. We are now looking for a Senior Product Data Analyst to lead the strategy for our Banking domain—the technical and financial heart of Shine’s value proposition.What's in it for you?Strategic Discovery: You will lead "Phase 0" of the product lifecycle, sizing opportunities for new banking features and identifying friction points in the user journey before a single line of code is written.End-to-End Experimentation: From power-calculating sample sizes to interpreting complex A/B test results, you own the validation of our product hypotheses.High-Stakes Influence: You will translate “Data-speak” into “Business-speak,” presenting findings directly to Product Leads and the Head of Product Analytics to help the company pivot or persevere on key initiatives.A Mature Stack: Work with a best-in-class modern data stack including Snowflake, dbt, Python, and Omni, supported by a dedicated Analytics Engineering team.A hybrid work policy: Enjoy a balanced mix of remote work and office collaborationModern, centrally located offices: Work from modern office spaces in prime city locationsAn international environment: Join a diverse team with colleagues from across the globeMobility across locations: Opportunity to work from our offices in Copenhagen, Paris, Amsterdam, or BerlinYour ResponsibilitiesStrategic Partnership: Partner directly with Banking Product Managers to define North Star KPIs and influence the long-term roadmap.Deep-Dive Analysis: Lead sophisticated analyses on user activation, transaction velocity, retention, and feature adoption within the banking ecosystem.Data Governance: Own and continuously improve analytical datasets and core Banking KPIs, ensuring reliability, scalability, and clear documentation.Enablement: Build actionable, self-service dashboards and mentor junior analysts to foster a high-performance data culture.Best Practices: Contribute to evolving our internal analytics standards, from SQL styling to peer-review processes.About YouExperience: 5+ years in Data Analytics, with a proven track record in a fast-paced, product-driven environment.Technical Excellence: Expert-level SQL and solid experience with DBT (modeling, testing, and documentation).Product Mindset: You think in terms of user value and business outcomes, not just "queries completed."Domain Expertise: Prior experience in Banking, FinTech, or regulated environments is highly valued.Communication: Ability to turn complex, ambiguous problems into clear, actionable recommendations for non-technical stakeholders.Language: Fluent in English; excited to work in a diverse, international setting.Our Recruitment ProcessScreening Call: Initial screening call with a TA-partnerTechnical Call (60’): Discussion with Saghar (Analytics Manager) + a technical SQL assessment.Case Study Presentation (60’): Evaluation of your methodology, problem-structuring, and impact mindset.Logical & Personality Assessment (30–45’): Followed by meeting a few team members + Discussion with the VP of Data & Analytics regarding collaboration and strategic vision.

title: Data Scientist / Analyst H/F
description: Votre future entreprise en quelques motsGroupama Assurances Mutuelles est à la fois l'organe central du Groupe Groupama et la caisse nationale de réassurance mutuelle agricole Groupama.Ainsi, Groupama Assurances Mutuelles anime et coordonne la stratégie opérationnelle du Groupe, en concertation avec les Caisses régionales et ses filiales françaises et internationales.Vos futures missionsAu sein la Direction Marketing Client de Groupama Assurances Mutuelles, l'équipe « Connaissance client prédictif » est un contributeur clé de l'orientation clients et de la personnalisation de nos communications à leur attention, sur la base des scores d'appétence et segmentation client .Elle est au cœur de la révolution technologique en marche avec l'Intelligence Artificielle au service de nos forces de vente et de l'ensemble des points de contacts (humains ou numériques).Concrètement, par ses travaux, elle permet de personnaliser des parcours et d'orienter l'exposition de nos clients aux sujets les plus pertinents pour eux, d'aider les commerciaux à faire les bons rebonds commerciaux, à proposer la bonne offre au bon moment avec le bon discours.Forte d'une grande maturité acquise depuis plus de 15 ans, cette équipe fait vivre un patrimoine de 40 scores et segmentations robustes, fréquemment renouvelé, et faisant la preuve régulière de leur pertinence notamment dans les campagnes du plan de contacts.Dans ce cadre, l'activité de Data Scientist au sein de cette équipe comprend les missionssuivantes :Réaliser les études et modèles liés à la connaissance clients : scoring prédictif score d'appétence à la souscription pour un client non équipé , segmentation, étude ad hoc sur les profiling client / prospect. ; ces projets comprennent la réalisation des bases de données, de la modélisation statistique, le suivi de performance et la proposition d'axes d'amélioration dans des environnements classiques (SAS, Altair, Datawhare et DataLab)..Apporter les réponses aux besoins de grands projets visant l'amélioration de nos interactions avec nos clients (Satisfaction et amélioration des expériences clients, - next best advice, Personnalisation espace client gfr,…) ; cela par le biais de modélisations, de machine learning, permettant par exemple d'anticiper des comportements clients futurs, en détectant les signaux faibles de fragilisation de la relation (par exemple dans leur navigation on line); de déceler des opportunités d'up-sell ou cross-sell, avec un moteur de recommandation sur le poste detravail du conseiller etcMaintenir et améliorer les scores ou segment du patrimoine, avec rigueurnécessaire pour réaliser les mises en production dans les outils Groupama.Présenter des documents pédagogiques synthétisant les travaux et les enseignementsAccompagner les équipes fonctionnelles (réseau distribution, digital, marketing) dans l'exploitation concrète des scores et segments,Gérer des projets dans l'univers Datalab de Groupama (DEV'IN) en bénéficiant d'un cadre agile et transverse au sein du GroupeVous vous reconnaissez dans ce profil ?Bac+5 en Statistiques : Master 2 spécialisé en statistiques ou data science : ENSAE, INSUP, ENSAI, Ecole d'ingénieur :Centrale, Polytechnique, Institut Mines Télécom, Télécom ParisTech, EFREI, les Universités spécialisées…Vous avez acquis une expérience de deux ans minimum.Compétences spécifiques requises indispensables :maîtrise des logiciels SAS, SAS GRID, , connaissance Dataikumaîtrise des langages (type Python plusieurs package ,…),SQL, Spark…Connaissance : Statistiques, datascience, scoringQualités recherchées :Doté(e) de qualités relationnelles, vous êtes enthousiaste, appréciez le travail en équipe et faites preuve de proactivité, d'écoute et d'arbitrage.Vous êtes rigoureux(se), structuré(e), vous avez un esprit analytique.Vous faites preuve de curiosité.Vos capacités d'analyse (faire parler les chiffres) et de synthèse seront des atouts dans la réussite de vos missions.

title: AI Research Scientist – Datadog AI Research (DAIR)
description: As a Research Scientist on our team, you will partner with Research Engineers, working on fundamental research problems and collaborating with Datadog's product and engineering teams to translate research advances into products.Building on our track record of AI-powered solutions (e.g., Bits AI, Bits Evolve, and our time series foundation model), Datadog AI Research tackles high-risk, high-reward problems grounded in real-world challenges in cloud observability and security.We are focused on two research areas:World Models for Observability -- Training multimodal foundation models that learn the joint dynamics of distributed systems across metrics, traces, logs, topology, and events. These models power advanced forecasting, anomaly detection, root cause analysis, counterfactual simulation ("what if?"), and provide a learned planning backbone for our autonomous agents.Trained Agents for Observability -- Post-training models to operate autonomously across Datadog's domain. SRE incident response is our first target, with a clear path to code repair, security response, and infrastructure optimization. We build the simulation environments, RL training loops, and evaluation infrastructure needed to train agents that match or surpass frontier models at a fraction of the cost.What You'll Do:Conduct research in generative AI and machine learning, building specialized foundation models and trained agents for observabilityTrain multimodal models on large-scale, diverse telemetry data (metrics, logs, traces, topology, events) using distributed training infrastructureDesign and build simulated environments and RL training loops for on-policy agent training and evaluationCollaborate with cross-functional teams (Product, Engineering) to integrate capabilities like multimodal world modeling and autonomous agents into Datadog's productsStay at the forefront of foundation models, world models, and RL-based agent researchContribute to research publications, present at top-tier conferences (e.g., NeurIPS, ICLR, ICML), and help open-source key model artifacts and benchmarksWho You Are:You hold a PhD in Computer Science, Machine Learning, or a related field, with deep expertise in areas like generative modeling, world models, AI agents, reinforcement learning, or multimodal learning (or have equivalent experience)You have extensive experience designing and implementing deep learning models and agents, with a strong background in distributed training frameworks (e.g., DeepSpeed, Megatron-LM) and ML libraries (PyTorch)You have a track record of impactful publications at top-tier venues (e.g., NeurIPS, ICLR, ICML, TMLR)You are familiar with efficient training, post-training, and inference techniques for large foundation modelsYou can explain complex models and research findings to both technical and non-technical audiencesBonus Points (any of the following):Experience bridging research and real-world product applications, especially with large foundation models, world models, or RL-trained agentsPassion for pushing the boundaries of AI with a focus on customer impact and scalable deploymentExperience writing production data pipelines and applicationsHands-on experience with GPU programming and optimization, including CUDADatadog values people from all walks of life. We understand not everyone will meet all the above qualifications on day one. That's okay. If you’re passionate about technology and want to grow your skills, we encourage you to apply.Benefits and Growth:Competitive global benefitsNew hire stock equity (RSUs) and employee stock purchase plan (ESPP)Opportunity to collaborate closely with colleagues across the Datadog offices in New York City and ParisOpportunity to attend and present at conferences and meetupsIntra-departmental mentor and buddy program for in-house networkingAn inclusive company culture, ability to join our Community Guilds (Datadog employee resource groups)Benefits and Growth listed above may vary based on the country of your employment and the nature of your employment with Datadog.About Datadog:Datadog (NASDAQ: DDOG) is a global SaaS business, delivering a rare combination of growth and profitability. We are on a mission to break down silos and solve complexity in the cloud age by enabling digital transformation, cloud migration, and infrastructure monitoring of our customers’ entire technology stacks. Built by engineers, for engineers, Datadog is used by organizations of all sizes across a wide range of industries. Together, we champion professional development, diversity of thought, innovation, and work excellence to empower continuous growth. Join the pack and become part of a collaborative, pragmatic, and thoughtful people-first community where we solve tough problems, take smart risks, and celebrate one another. Learn more about #DatadogLife on Instagram, LinkedIn, and Datadog Learning Center.Equal Opportunity at Datadog:Datadog is an Affirmative Action and Equal Opportunity Employer and is proud to offer equal employment opportunity to everyone regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity, veteran status, and more. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. Here are our Candidate Legal Notices for your reference.Your Privacy:Any information you submit to Datadog as part of your application will be processed in accordance with Datadog’s Applicant and Candidate Privacy Notice.About Datadog:Datadog (NASDAQ: DDOG) is a global SaaS business, delivering a rare combination of growth and profitability.
